keyFindings: White House-affiliated activists circulated 17-page draft emergency executive order to declare national emergency over elections, ban mail-in ballots, and require voter re-registration with proof of citizenship ahead of 2026 midterms (Washington Post)
DOJ sued five additional states in March 2026 for refusing to hand over voter registration lists, bringing total to 29 states plus D.C.; New Jersey AG called the request 'baseless' (DOJ/UPI)
SAVE America Act reached 50 Republican Senate votes but remains filibuster-blocked; Senate Majority Leader Thune acknowledged 'no path' through talking filibuster (NBC News)

Minnesota Chief Judge Schiltz escalated to criminal contempt threat against ICE officials after documenting 209+ ICE court order violations in 151 cases; stated 'One way or another, ICE will comply' (Minnesota Reformer)
Reuters investigation documented 600+ social media posts targeting family members of judges, with 70+ explicitly calling for violence; campaign viewed 200+ million times (Reuters)
DOJ failed to file by Court of International Trade deadline on $175-200B tariff refunds; AG Bondi called judges 'out of control' and said they 'need to be removed' (CNBC)
Pentagon removed Vice Admiral Fred Kacher as Joint Staff Director after fewer than 90 days, continuing purge of senior military leadership including Chairman, CNO, Air Force Chief (U.S. News)
CISA's third acting director installed after previous removal Feb 27; agency has operated without Senate-confirmed director for over a year while workforce cut by one-third (TechCrunch)
CBO estimated domestic military deployments cost $496M through December 2025, running at $93M/month into 2026; legal challenges under Posse Comitatus Act ongoing (Lawfare)
Schedule F final rule took effect March 8, 2026, reclassifying ~50,000 federal positions to lose civil service protections and whistleblower protections (Federal News Network)
D.C. Circuit heard oral arguments Feb 24 on CFPB's 88% workforce reduction with court appearing divided; major regulatory agency remains in legal limbo (Government Executive)
EPA endangerment finding repeal lawsuits filed by coalition including American Lung Association; SCOTUS ruling on independent agency heads expected by June 2026 (Earthjustice)
Section 122 global import surcharge took effect Feb 24 at 15% with hard 150-day limit expiring July 24; Foreign Policy argues use is illegal (Federal Register)
EU froze ratification of 'Turnberry' trade agreement and threatened Anti-Coercion Instrument targeting $22.5B retaliation list; India paused interim trade deal (Al Jazeera)
Court of International Trade facing prolonged litigation over $175-200B in IEEPA refunds; Trump claimed at State of Union he could impose 'far worse' terms (Fortune)
Federal judge forced government to reveal DOGE employee names; Maryland judge ruled Musk cannot be shielded from questions about USAID dissolution (Bloomberg)
PBS analysis found nearly 40% of 1,000+ contracts cancelled by DOGE totaling $8.6B expected to produce no savings; federal spending increased overall (PBS)
Rep. Sherrill called for IG investigations at eight agencies into Musk/DOGE conflicts; Musk's companies could avoid $2B liability through government role (The Hill)
DHS partial shutdown ongoing since Feb 14 with TSA workers facing first missed paycheck March 14, raising prospect of walkouts (Fox5DC)
Kevin Warsh Fed nomination faces 'mounting political obstacles' with Powell's term expiring May 15; grand jury investigation of Powell unprecedented (Bloomberg)
Congressional layoff moratorium expired Feb 27 amid DHS shutdown; DOGE cuts estimated to cost taxpayers $135B through paid leave and re-hiring (Government Executive)
Seventeen nonprofits filed amicus brief urging D.C. Circuit to block retaliatory FTC investigation into Media Matters after X/Nazi content reporting (The Intercept)
Don Lemon prosecution proceeding on federal charges for livestreaming church protest; FIRE and Amnesty condemned charges as 'autocratic playbook' (FIRE)
Corporation for Public Broadcasting dissolution continuing with nationwide staff cuts; FBI raid on Washington Post reporter drew judicial rebuke (NBC News)
Reuters documented 600+ posts targeting federal judges' families with 70+ explicitly calling for violence; campaign viewed 200+ million times with Musk amplification (Reuters)
At least 33 pardoned January 6 defendants rearrested for additional crimes including child sexual assault and conspiracy to murder FBI agents (19th News)
Federal agents killed two U.S. citizens in Minneapolis during immigration enforcement; DHS admitted agents 'appear to have lied under oath' about separate shooting (NPR)
Congressional layoff moratorium expired Feb 27; GAO data shows 134,000 employees (6% of workforce) separated Jan-June 2025 while only 66,000 hired (GAO)
CFPB refused to cooperate with GAO investigation of 88% workforce reduction including 90% of supervision and 80% of enforcement divisions (GAO)
Over 75% of inspector general positions remain vacant; 8,000+ government web pages and 3,000 datasets removed from public access (Marketplace)
Trump publicly rebuked Apple's Tim Cook for India manufacturing, then granted tariff protection after Apple pledged U.S. investment, showing trade policy leverage (WSJ)
Threatened to sue JPMorgan Chase for 'debanking' conservatives; criticized major banks for allegedly discriminating against political viewpoints (WaPo)
'Freedom 250' initiative created pay-to-play dynamic allowing corporations with regulatory interests to make tax-deductible contributions tied to presidential access (NYT)
